title: T-052 — E2E happy path: approved WO start → consume → output → complete → D365 enqueue (§8.2.1-8.2.4)
prompt:
# T-052 — E2E happy path: approved WO → D365 enqueue

PRD: docs/prd/08-PRODUCTION-PRD.md §8.2.1, §8.2.2, §8.2.3, §8.2.4, §1.1A, §7.5 (Epic E5 INTEGRATIONS)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Playwright spec covering the full happy path on an approved WO: START preflight admits, scanner consume-to-WO succeeds, output registration with catch-weight, COMPLETE passes `closed_production_strict_v1`, and `production_outbox_events` row is enqueued with `status='pending'` (dispatcher delivery proven via T-041 unit tests, not asserted live here). Asserts D365 metadata is never the source-of-truth for admission.

## Implementation contract
1. apps/web/e2e/production.spec.ts adds `test.describe('happy path')` block that walks the UI from `/production` → WO detail → consume tab → output tab → complete dialog.
2. apps/web/e2e/fixtures/production.ts seeds: a tenant, a line, an operator with PIN, a canonical release row `released_to_factory` for `active_bom_header_id` + `active_factory_spec_id`, a WO snapshot referencing those IDs, and an LP available for consumption.
3. Assertions verify: (a) UI state transitions (status badge IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED), (b) DB rows in `wo_executions`, `wo_material_consumption`, `wo_outputs`, `production_outbox_events`, (c) no canonical release row was mutated, (d) `data-prototype-label` markers present at each step.
4. Playwright config keeps `trace='on'`, `screenshot='only-on-failure'` and `video='retain-on-failure'`; outputs land under `apps/web/test-results/`.
5. Clean up tenant-scoped data after each run (org-scoped helper in fixtures).

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a tenant with a `released_to_factory` WO snapshot, when the happy-path Playwright test runs end-to-end, then UI shows IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED, `wo_executions.completed_at` is set, exactly one `production_outbox_events` row of `event_type='wo.completed'` is created in `status='pending'`, and the canonical release row is unchanged.
2. Given the spec finishes (pass or fail), when artifacts are inspected, then `apps/web/test-results/` contains the trace.zip, screenshot(s), and a video for the run.
3. Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect evidence, then closeout lists trace/screenshot/video artifact paths plus the `pnpm --filter web exec playwright test production.spec.ts --trace=on` command output.

## Test strategy
- RED: write apps/web/e2e/production.spec.ts `happy path` describe + fixtures before any UI/API slice exists; the spec must fail with `wo_executions` missing or release admit returning blockers.
- Run `pnpm --filter web exec playwright test production.spec.ts -g "happy path" --trace=on`
- Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not treat D365 sync/export/Built as production release source-of-truth in fixtures.
- Do not skip canonical release row seeding (no shortcuts via D365 metadata).
- Do not leak test data across tenants — always clean up by tenant_id.
- Do not assert live D365 delivery in this spec (covered by T-041 dispatcher unit tests).

pipeline_inputs.description: Playwright happy-path spec across approved WO start/consume/output/complete + D365 outbox enqueue; canonical release row never mutated.
pipeline_inputs.details: Single describe block walking /production → WO detail → consume → output → complete. Fixtures seed canonical release row (released_to_factory), WO snapshot, LP and operator PIN per tenant. Assertions cover UI status transitions, DB row inserts in wo_executions/wo_material_consumption/wo_outputs/production_outbox_events, no canonical mutation, and data-prototype-label markers at each step. Playwright config retains trace/screenshot/video. Tenant-scoped cleanup after each run.
out_of_scope: Do not assert live D365 delivery (covered by T-041)
Do not implement UI surfaces (T-046, T-047 own those)
acceptance_criteria: Given a tenant with a released_to_factory WO snapshot, when the happy-path Playwright test runs end-to-end, then UI shows IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED, wo_executions.completed_at is set, exactly one production_outbox_events row of event_type='wo.completed' is created in status='pending', and the canonical release row is unchanged.
Given the spec finishes, when artifacts are inspected, then apps/web/test-results/ contains the trace.zip, screenshot(s), and a video for the run.
Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect evidence, then closeout lists trace/screenshot/video artifact paths plus the playwright test command output.

Given an LP/lot has an active quality hold (v_active_holds), when WO consume is attempted, then the operation is rejected with 409 quality_hold_active and an outbox event production.consume.blocked is emitted (consume gate per 09-quality T-064).
test_strategy: RED: write apps/web/e2e/production.spec.ts happy-path describe + fixtures before any UI/API slice exists; spec must fail with wo_executions missing or release admit returning blockers.
Run 'pnpm --filter web exec playwright test production.spec.ts -g "happy path" --trace=on'
Capture screenshots and Playwright trace/video/artifacts; attach paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not treat D365 sync/export/Built as production release source-of-truth in fixtures.
Do not skip canonical release row seeding.
Do not leak test data across tenants.
Do not assert live D365 delivery in this spec.
Do not bypass the 09-quality consume gate (T-064 v_active_holds) — every consume path must call holdsGuard(lpId, lotId) before mutating consumption tables.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Canonical factory release read-model owner; fixture must seed against this contract.
Technical BOM/factory_spec approval contract; fixture must seed valid active IDs.

Quality consume gate (v_active_holds) — every LP/WO consume operation must call holdsGuard(lpId, lotId) before mutating consumption/output state. Active hold returns 409 quality_hold_active and emits production.consume.blocked outbox event (PRD §16.4 V-PROD-02/V-PROD-16).
